# Models

The engine routes models to OpenAI, Anthropic, or OpenRouter based on the model string you provide in a `CognitiveStep` or blueprint default.

## Model aliases

Use these aliases to stay stable while we evolve underlying models:

- `fast` → `gpt-5-mini` (OpenAI)
- `quality` → `gpt-5.2` (OpenAI)
- `vision` → `gpt-5-mini` (OpenAI)
- `claude` → `claude-4.5-sonnet` (Anthropic)
- `sonnet` → `claude-sonnet-4-5` (Anthropic)
- `opus` → `claude-opus-4-5` (Anthropic)
- `haiku` → `claude-haiku-4-5` (Anthropic)

```javascript
const [nextMemory] = await externalDialog(workingMemory, "say hi", { model: "quality" })
```

We generally recommend using `fast` for internal thought processes and `quality` for user-facing dialog.

## Model routing rules

- Any model string starting with `gpt` routes to OpenAI.
- Any model string that is not an alias and does not start with `gpt` routes through OpenRouter.

This means you can try new models without a code change. For example:

```javascript
const [, reply] = await externalDialog(workingMemory, "say hi", { model: "moonshotai/kimi-k2-0905" })
```

## Default model

When a blueprint does not have a `defaultModel` specified, the default model is `fast`.

You can set `defaultModel` in the package.json of your soul by adding a `"soulEngine"` field:

```js filename="package.json"
{
  // ... the rest of your package.json
  "soulEngine": {
    "defaultModel": "quality"
  }
}
```

Now when you use a cognitiveStep without a model specified, your soul will choose `quality` instead of `fast`.

## Vision model for images

Use the `vision` alias to target an image-capable model:

```typescript
const imageUrl = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/pt/6/6f/HopperAutomat.jpg";

const withImageUrl = workingMemory.withMemory({
  role: ChatMessageRoleEnum.User,
  content: [
    {
      type: "image_url",
      image_url: {
        url: imageUrl,
      },
    },
  ],
})

const [, imageDescription] = await instruction(withImageUrl, "describe the image", { model: "vision" })

log("Image description:", imageDescription);
```
